title: History of Anesthesia MCQ – Chapter 8
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav

questions.question: Who used the term 'anesthesia' in 1st AD to describe the narcotic effects of the plant Mandragora?
questions.explanation: Page 83: History of anesthesia dates back to 1st AD when Dioscorides used the term anesthesia to describe the narcotic effects of the plant Mandragora.

questions.question: The word 'anesthesia', which means no senses, was coined by:
questions.explanation: Page 83: The word anesthesia, which means no senses, was coined by Oliver Wendell Holmes.

questions.question: The term 'balanced anesthesia' was coined by:
questions.explanation: Page 83: The term 'balanced anesthesia' was coined by John Lundy and Ralph Waters.

questions.question: Which was the first anesthetic gas, and who synthesized it in 1774?
questions.explanation: Page 83: The first anesthetic gas, i.e. nitrous oxide, was synthesized by Priestley in 1774 (Priestley also synthesized oxygen, which he called Dephlogisticated air).

questions.question: Who first used nitrous oxide on himself for a toothache and called it 'laughing gas'?
questions.explanation: Page 83: The first clinical use of nitrous oxide was done by Humphry Davy, who used it on himself for toothache and called nitrous oxide as 'laughing gas'.

questions.question: The first public clinical demonstration of nitrous oxide (1845) was given by Horace Wells for what procedure?
questions.explanation: Page 83: The first public clinical demonstration of nitrous oxide was given by Horace Wells for tooth extraction (1845), but unfortunately the patient cried in pain.

questions.question: Horace Wells became addicted to which agent and later committed suicide by cutting his femoral artery?
questions.explanation: Page 83: Horace Wells became so frustrated that he became a chloroform addict and committed suicide by cutting his femoral artery.

questions.question: Ether was prepared by Valerius Cordus in 1540 and was known by what name?
questions.explanation: Page 83: Ether was prepared by Valerius Cordus in 1540 when it was known as 'sweet oil of vitriol'.

questions.question: Who gave the first public demonstration of ether anesthesia on 16th October 1846?
questions.explanation: Page 83: The first public demonstration of ether was given by WTG Morton on 16th October 1846, and that is why 16th October is celebrated as World Anesthesia Day.

questions.question: Which date is celebrated as World Anesthesia Day?
questions.explanation: Page 83: The first public demonstration of ether was given by WTG Morton on 16th October 1846, and that is why 16th October is celebrated as World Anesthesia Day.

questions.question: The first successful demonstration of ether anesthesia by Morton took place at which hospital (Fig. 8.1)?
questions.explanation: Page 83, Fig. 8.1: The first successful demonstration of ether anesthesia was by Morton in Massachusetts General Hospital.

questions.question: Who popularized chloroform and used it on Queen Victoria for the birth of her 8th child?
questions.explanation: Page 84: It was John Snow who popularized chloroform, using it successfully in 4,000 patients. He used chloroform on Queen Victoria for the birth of her 8th child.

questions.question: In how many patients did John Snow use chloroform successfully?
questions.explanation: Page 84: It was John Snow who popularized chloroform, using it successfully in 4,000 patients.

questions.question: The first intravenous anesthetic used was thiopentone. Who used it and in which year?
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first intravenous anesthetic used was thiopentone by John Lundy in 1934.

questions.question: The first local anesthetic used was cocaine. Who used it and for what purpose in 1884?

options.A: Carl Koller, for anesthetizing the cornea
options.B: J Leonard Corning, for spinal anesthesia

questions.explanation: Page 84: The first local anesthetic used was cocaine by Carl Koller for anesthetizing the cornea (1884).

questions.question: Who first gave spinal anesthesia in dogs?
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first spinal anesthesia in dogs was given by J Leonard Corning, while the first spinal anesthesia in human beings was given by August Bier in 1898.

questions.question: The first spinal anesthesia in human beings was given by August Bier in which year?
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first spinal anesthesia in human beings was given by August Beir (Bier) in 1898.

questions.question: The first epidural anesthesia (1901) was given by:
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first epidural anesthesia was given by Cathelin and Sicard (1901).

questions.question: Who was the first to use 'curare' for muscle relaxation in 1942?
questions.explanation: Page 84: Harold Griffith was the first to use 'curare' for muscle relaxation (1942).

questions.question: Bovet received the Nobel Prize (1949) for using which muscle relaxant in practice?
questions.explanation: Page 84: Bovet got the Nobel Prize (1949) for using Suxamethonium (Succinylcholine) in practice.

questions.question: Who received the Nobel Prize in 1949 in connection with muscle relaxants?
questions.explanation: Page 84: Bovet got the Nobel Prize (1949) for using Suxamethonium (Succinylcholine) in practice.

questions.question: The first Boyle's machine was invented in 1917 by:
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first Boyle's machine was invented by Edmund Gaskin Boyle in 1917.

questions.question: The first endotracheal intubation was done by:
questions.explanation: Page 84: The first endotracheal intubation was done by Ivan Magill.

questions.question: Apart from synthesizing nitrous oxide, Priestley is also credited with synthesizing which other gas, which he called 'Dephlogisticated air'?
questions.explanation: Page 83: Priestley also synthesized oxygen, which he called Dephlogisticated air.

questions.question: Ether anesthesia in Morton's first demonstration was used for removal of what?
questions.explanation: Page 100: First public demonstration of ether was given by WTG Morton on 16th October 1846 for the removal of jaw tumor.
